notes: Codex emits an in-band irrlicht-eta marker (driven by an AGENTS.md rule it loads verbatim) and irrlicht SHOULD parse it read-only: while working, session metrics carry task_estimate {total_rounds:4, completed_rounds:M} matching the marker (completed_rounds<=total_rounds), updated_at advances on each fresher marker, completed_rounds is non-decreasing and reaches 4 on the final working turn, task_completion_eta is present once completed_rounds>=1 and burns down (later eta <= earlier), and both fields are absent at ready (suppressed-by-state). These metric assertions are NOT expressible in the phase DSL today (cf. codex/basic-turn spec); they are asserted in the recipe verify[] and covered by the claudecode taskestimate_test.go unit tests. The phases below assert the user-observable STATE arc the marker rides on (ready->working->ready). KNOWN-FAILING in v1: the codex adapter has no scanTaskEstimate (claudecode-only; core/adapters/inbound/agents/codex has no taskestimate.go), so task_estimate is never produced for codex regardless of emission. The spec asserts the CORRECT behavior the per-adapter port must satisfy.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch (AGENTS.md with the marker rule already seeded in the cwd)
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready
First prompt opens the 4-phase task -> working; codex emits the initial marker (completed_rounds=0, total_rounds=4) — task_estimate should be present while working (currently absent: known_failing)

Across the 'next' turns completed_rounds climbs non-decreasing to 4 and task_completion_eta burns down; the final working turn reports the task done, then the session settles back to ready where task_estimate / task_completion_eta are absent (suppressed-by-state)
Driver tears down the REPL — process_exited on the rollout session after state returned to ready
